topic: India Superlatives — Largest, Longest, Highest & Records

questions.text: Which is the largest state in India by area?
questions.explanation: Rajasthan is the largest state in India by area (~3.42 lakh sq km). Madhya Pradesh was the largest state until 2000, when Chhattisgarh was carved out of it. Rajasthan is home to the Thar Desert, which is India's largest desert.

questions.text: The oldest national park in India is:
questions.explanation: Jim Corbett National Park (established 1936 as Hailey National Park) is India's oldest national park, located in Uttarakhand. It was the first to come under Project Tiger in 1973. Hemis National Park in Ladakh is the largest national park in India.

questions.text: The highest dam in India is:
questions.explanation: Tehri Dam in Uttarakhand (on Bhagirathi river) is India's highest dam at 260.5 metres. Bhakra Dam (Himachal Pradesh) is the 2nd highest. Hirakud Dam in Odisha is the longest dam (~26 km). Tehri is also the 8th highest dam in the world.

questions.text: The Statue of Unity, the world's tallest statue, is dedicated to:
questions.explanation: The Statue of Unity, at 182 metres (597 feet), is dedicated to Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel — the 'Iron Man of India.' It is located in Kevadia (Ekta Nagar), Gujarat, near the Narmada Dam. It was inaugurated on October 31, 2018 (Sardar Patel's birthday).

Chilika Lake is the largest coastal lagoon in Asia and the largest brackish water lake in India. It is NOT the largest freshwater lake (that's Wular Lake, J&K) or saltwater lake (that's Sambhar, Rajasthan). Chilika is a Ramsar wetland and a major flamingo habitat.

questions.text: India's longest road bridge, the Bhupen Hazarika Setu, is located over which river?
questions.explanation: The Dhola-Sadiya Bridge (named Bhupen Hazarika Setu after the legendary singer), at 9.15 km, is India's longest road bridge. It crosses the Lohit river (a tributary of the Brahmaputra) in Assam, connecting Assam to Arunachal Pradesh.

questions.text: The Atal Tunnel (Rohtang) holds which distinction?
options: World's longest highway tunnel above 10,000 feet
questions.explanation: Atal Tunnel (Rohtang) at 9.02 km is the world's longest highway tunnel above 10,000 feet altitude, connecting Manali to Leh through the Pir Panjal range. It was inaugurated by PM Modi in October 2020. The Pir Panjal Tunnel is India's longest railway tunnel (J&K).

questions.text: Which state has the highest literacy rate in India (Census 2011)?
questions.explanation: Kerala has the highest overall literacy rate (~94%, Census 2011). Mizoram has the highest literacy rate among NE states and also the highest percentage of forest cover (~86%). These two are frequently confused.

questions.text: India's Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad holds which distinction?
options: World's largest cricket stadium by capacity
questions.explanation: Narendra Modi Stadium (formerly Sardar Patel Stadium) in Motera, Ahmedabad has a capacity of 1,32,000 and is the world's largest cricket stadium by seating capacity. It was renovated and renamed in 2020.

questions.text: The wettest place in India (and the world) is:
questions.explanation: Mawsynram in Meghalaya is the wettest place in India and the world (~11,871 mm average annual rainfall). Cherrapunji (also in Meghalaya) held the record earlier and still holds records for extreme rainfall events. Both are often confused.

questions.text: India overtook China as the world's most populous country in approximately which year?
questions.explanation: According to UN estimates, India surpassed China as the world's most populous country around April 2023, with approximately 1.429 billion people. India's population continues to grow while China's has stabilised due to its one-child policy legacy.

questions.text: The Atal Setu (Mumbai Trans Harbour Link), India's longest sea bridge, was inaugurated in:
questions.explanation: The Atal Setu (Mumbai Trans Harbour Link), at 21.8 km, was inaugurated on January 12, 2024 by PM Narendra Modi. It connects Sewri (Mumbai) to Nhava (Uran, Raigad) and is India's longest sea bridge, reducing travel time from 1 hour to 20 minutes.

questions.text: The largest national park in India by area is:
questions.explanation: Hemis National Park in Ladakh, at ~4,400 sq km, is the largest national park in India. It is famous for the snow leopard. Jim Corbett NP is the oldest. Ranthambore is famous for tigers. Kanha is in Madhya Pradesh.

questions.text: The longest national highway in India (NH-44) runs between:
questions.explanation: NH-44 (formerly NH-7 and parts of NH-1 combined under new numbering) runs approximately 3,745 km from Srinagar (J&K) to Kanyakumari (Tamil Nadu), making it India's longest national highway.

questions.text: Wular Lake is the largest freshwater lake in India. It is located in:
questions.explanation: Wular Lake in Jammu & Kashmir is the largest freshwater lake in India and one of the largest in Asia. It is connected to the Jhelum River and is a Ramsar Wetland. Dalal Begum, on the banks of Wular, is also historically significant.

questions.text: India's economy is currently ranked _____ in the world by nominal GDP (2024):
questions.explanation: India is ranked 5th in the world by nominal GDP (2024), having overtaken the UK in 2022. By Purchasing Power Parity (PPP), India ranks 3rd (after China and USA). India is the fastest-growing major economy and is expected to become the 3rd largest by nominal GDP soon.
